title: Best subtitles for Evangelion TV series
display_title: Best subtitles for Evangelion TV series
wikitext:
[]: Best subtitles for Evangelion TV series?             Quote Post by hersuri » Thu Oct 06, 2011 7:20 am Hi, I've only ever experienced Eva with the ADV subs and dub - after comprehensively reading the EvaGeeks commentary project, it has lead me to believe that these subs might not be entirely accurate or consistent with the series. Have there been any other groups that have provided more accurate or fitting subtitles?

[]:             Quote Post by Deepak » Thu Oct 06, 2011 8:18 am platinum subs are accepted by most people, though is has some error. The literal translation project is also a very good resource.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Thu Oct 06, 2011 9:49 am Deepak wrote: platinum subs are accepted by most people, though is has some error.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Oct 06, 2011 12:26 pm The Platinum subs are functional , though I can't help but feel that their academic dryness frequently undermines the emotionally loaded comtent and eloquence of the show. It'd be nice if there were subs that were similarly accurate but prosaically superior.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Thu Oct 06, 2011 1:52 pm very good topic , I have only the platinum subs to go on, but they work just fine for me. I think the concern with both the sub's and dub's is localization of the translated text in that regard all translations of eva suffer from this, my advice would be just to go with the newest translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Deepak » Thu Oct 06, 2011 5:15 pm Dr. Nick wrote: Such as?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Oct 06, 2011 8:26 pm Nitpicks aside, ADV's dub is marginal at best but the sub translations are pretty faithful overall. It loses some of its eloquence, but everything does when you translate it into a different language.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Oct 06, 2011 9:26 pm I think the dub, like most halfway-decent ones, deserves credit for reworking the translated script into dialogue that sounds reasonably like stuff real people might say, even if it did have more than a few flubs. (Also keep in mind that they were working off an older, rougher translation than for the Platinum subs; I recall reading that Matt Greenfield said if he were to do the dub over again he would script and direct a lot of things differently, and some particularly erratic lines actually were re-recorded for Platinum, though I don't have details on exactly which ones.)

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Oct 07, 2011 10:37 pm The dub is actually quite good considering the era in which it was made, it just doesn't hold up very well today.

[]:             Quote Post by hersuri » Sat Oct 08, 2011 10:44 am So Platinum is the way to go for subs?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Oct 08, 2011 12:40 pm Trajan wrote: The dub is actually quite good considering the era in which it was made, it just doesn't hold up very well today.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 08, 2011 12:56 pm hersuri wrote: So Platinum is the way to go for subs?

[]:             Quote Post by hersuri » Mon Jan 09, 2012 9:54 am Is there any particular resource to acquire deviating/varying subs from?

[]:             Quote Post by Deepak » Mon Jan 09, 2012 8:50 pm hersuri wrote: Is there any particular resource to acquire deviating/varying subs from?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 09, 2012 9:17 pm Those are all transcripts and not sub files per se.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:27 am Dr. Nick wrote: Such as?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:50 am Bagheera wrote: OT: The Platinum subs are the best. I don't know what people are on about when they say they're dry or stilted, since they read as well as the dub ever did to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:55 am symbv wrote: Potential issue is we are not entirely sure how accurate it is as translation of the Japanese scripts.....

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jan 10, 2012 11:04 am Bagheera wrote: True. It is the best available, but that does not mean it is perfect.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Jan 10, 2012 2:36 pm Heh. How long has the EGF commentary project been running again? I realize that project presents its own specialized difficulty but seriously, one should not expect much productivity from Eva fandom...the fact that this show which has been out for seventeen years has not already got a "canonical" subtitle project speaks volumes by itself. Plus, any new attempt to subtitle the series will inevitably disintegrate into the proper interpretations of lines that even Anno doesn't understand, and probably break up the subbing group into lol opinions and general bad feeling. Maybe if you got like the very best of the fansubbing community to work on the project for half a year you could get something done, but it's probably more sensible to just wait for the BD release some time ninety years from now.
summary:
[]: Best subtitles for Evangelion TV series?             Quote Post by hersuri » Thu Oct 06, 2011 7:20 am Hi, I've only ever experienced Eva with the ADV subs and dub - after comprehensively reading the EvaGeeks commentary project, it has lead me to believe that these subs might not be entirely accurate or consistent with the series. Have there been any other groups that have provided more accurate or fitting subtitles?

[]:  &nbsp
